forecast-period.text: A significant low pressure system west of TAS continues to move east whilst a second weak low pressure system moves over waters to the south today extending a cold front over coastal parts later today and into Thursday. A high pressure system over WA extends a ridge over western and northern parts on Thursday, before it moves into western SA on Friday, as a cold front embedded in a southwesterly flow clips the south. The high pressure system continues to move slowly eastwards over the weekend, reaching the Tasman Sea late Sunday evening.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers about the West Coast and agricultural area, possibly reaching the south of the Pastoral districts. Showers most likely about windward coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures below average grading to slightly above average in the north. West to southwesterly winds, turning southwest to southerly during the day. Fresh to strong and gusty about far southern coasts.

Showers about the West Coast and agricultural area, possibly reaching the south of the Pastoral districts. Possible thunderstorms near western coasts, Yorke Peninsula and the Mid North. Fresh and gusty westerly winds, tending southwesterly about coast parts during the evening.

Isolated fog patches in the west. Showers about the west coast and agricultural areas, mainly about windward coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures below average though near average about the agricultural area. Southwest to southerly winds, shifting west to southwesterly about the agricultural area during the afternoon and evening.

Isolated fog patches in the west. Light showers about the southern agricultural area, contracting to southern coasts during the afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures slightly above average grading to below average in the far north. Light winds tending northeast to northerly about the north and west and westerly about the southeast.

text.p: Isolated fog patches about central and eastern districts. Light showers about coastal parts, southern ranges and the southeast, contracting to southern coasts during the afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures slightly above average grading to below average in the far north. Light winds tending northeast to northerly during the day.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Sunday: Less than 5 mm about the south of the Pastoral districts, increasing to 2 to 10 mm about the agricultural area and Flinders district, and 10 to 25 mm about central and southern windward coasts and ranges.
